mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 1.5% in annualized forward returns over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A peak and subsequent decline in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks resolving into oversupply, which compresses corporate pricing power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns following the trigger remain positive in more than 60% of observations across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically lifting forward equity returns over the next 5 trading days as analysts revise estimates upward.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between GDP growth surprises and 5-day SPX returns drops below 0.10 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate raises borrowing costs and reduces equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive average forward returns in more than 50% of triggered episodes over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market imbalances that trigger wage-push inflation, forcing the Federal Reserve to tighten policy and depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS crosses above 131500 and UNRATE crosses above 4.8%
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward returns against the two series over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the consumer price index erodes real disposable income and suppresses discretionary spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings growth expectations downgrade.
failure_condition: Forward returns following the inflation trigger are not statistically different from zero (t-stat < 1.5) across a 3-year rolling window.
